title: How to Charge Crystals: Meaning, Methods & When to Do It
concept_focus: charge_vs_cleanse_only（局部对比，比喻：清空杯子→再倒水；不重复H1完整四概念表）+ cleanse first charge second 顺序链路

local_concept_pair.metaphor: cleanse = emptying a used cup; charge = refilling it with fresh water (不用H1的电池比喻)
local_concept_pair.order_rule: cleanse first, charge second — 顺序不可逆（先清后充）
local_concept_pair.link_to_h1: For the full four-concept comparison (Cleanse, Charge, Activate, Program), see How to Cleanse Crystals

methods_subset: charge subset (6 methods, no water/salt/smoke — charge 不用水盐烟)

tier1_safe.label: 🟢 Tier 1 — Safest & Recommended for All Stones
tier1_safe.subtitle: 最安全通用，新手首选 / 首饰无接触
narrative_variant: 满月充能叙事(最强charge时刻)

tier2_conditional.label: 🟡 Tier 2 — Conditional, Check Your Stone First
tier2_conditional.subtitle: 有条件，先查阳光耐受性

tier3_caution.label: 🔴 Tier 3 — Higher Risk / Niche, Use with Care
tier3_caution.subtitle: 高风险/小众，谨慎使用

moonlight.name: Moonlight
moonlight.tradition: The full moon is considered the most potent time to recharge a stone's symbolic energy — a monthly refill tied to the lunar cycle.
moonlight.practical: Moonlight is reflected sunlight at roughly 1/400,000 the intensity — gentle enough for every mineral, including color-sensitive stones that must avoid direct sun.
steps: Place crystals on a windowsill or covered porch at sunset
Leave overnight (6–12 hours); the full moon is ideal
Bring in before sunrise to avoid dew or direct sun
moonlight.best_for_default: every stone — especially color-sensitive quartz (amethyst, rose quartz) that must avoid direct sun, and a first charge for a new piece. The full moon is the classic charging moment in many traditions.

selenite.name: Selenite Plate / Bowl
selenite.tradition: Selenite is widely considered self-cleansing and is said to keep stones placed on it 'full' — a passive overnight recharge.
selenite.practical: No chemical interaction — completely harmless. Handle gently, as selenite itself is soft (Mohs 2).
steps: Place crystals on a selenite plate, bowl, or bar
Avoid dragging hard stones across the selenite surface
selenite.best_for_default: jewelry above all — bracelets, necklaces, and rings rest on the plate with zero water, salt, or handling. Also ideal for soft and porous stones that need a no-contact method.

sound.name: Sound (Singing Bowl / Bell)
sound.tradition: Sustained sound vibrations are believed to restore a stone's energy — a fast recharge for a whole collection.
sound.practical: Sound waves are far too low-energy to affect any mineral's structure — harmless to every stone.
steps: Play a singing bowl, bell, or tuning fork near the crystals
Works for your whole collection at once
sound.best_for_default: when you need speed and have many pieces — a singing bowl or bell recharges an entire collection at once in minutes. A practical choice for soft stones where you'd rather avoid any handling.

sunlight.name: Sunlight (Indirect / Brief)
sunlight.tradition: The sun's energy is associated with vitality and warmth — a midday recharge for sun-tolerant stones.
sunlight.practical: Direct sun can fade color-sensitive quartz (amethyst, rose quartz, citrine) and cause heat stress even in hard stones. Use indirect light or brief morning sun only.
steps: Confirm your stone tolerates sun (citrine, clear quartz, tiger's eye, black tourmaline — NOT amethyst/rose quartz/fluorite)
Place in indirect sunlight or brief morning sun (1–2 hours max)
Never leave color-sensitive stones in direct sun
sunlight.best_for_default: only sun-stable stones (citrine, clear quartz, tiger's eye, black tourmaline) — and even then, indirect or brief morning sun only. Never use direct sun on amethyst, rose quartz, or fluorite (color fades).

earth.name: Earth Burial
earth.tradition: Burying crystals returns them to the earth to absorb and restore energy — a deep grounding recharge.
earth.practical: Soil moisture and organic acids can stain or etch soft, porous stones — always wrap in cloth first.
steps: Wrap the stone in a natural cloth
Bury in soil (garden or houseplant pot)
Brush off soil gently when done
earth.best_for_default: high-hardness raw stones (Mohs 7+) with no metal settings — a deep grounding ritual for those drawn to burying a stone in soil overnight. Wrap the stone in natural cloth first, and keep jewelry pieces away from this method.

cluster.name: Crystal Cluster
cluster.tradition: Resting a stone on a large crystal cluster (clear quartz or amethyst) is said to recharge it through the cluster's collective energy.
cluster.practical: No chemical interaction — harmless to every stone. The cluster is a durable, passive charging base.
steps: Place a large clear quartz or amethyst cluster on a stable surface
Rest smaller stones on or around the cluster
Leave 24+ hours for a full recharge
cluster.best_for_default: collectors who already own a large cluster — a passive, decorative charging base that also doubles as a display. Works for every stone with no risk.

main_commercial.reason: Color-stable (iron + heat) and sun-tolerant — the natural fit for sunlight charging, plus a warm bright accent in any collection
main_commercial.compliance_phrase: Often associated with warmth and sunlight — a piece many enjoy as a bright accent in a collection

semantic_match.reason: Traditionally self-cleansing and used as a plate to recharge other stones — the natural mascot of the charging topic
semantic_match.compliance_phrase: A self-cleansing stone many use as a display plate to rest other pieces on — a practical care choice

entry_price.reason: Durable (Mohs 7), sun-tolerant, and a neutral staple — pairs with any charging method, beginner-friendly
entry_price.compliance_phrase: A versatile piece collectors often describe as a neutral staple that pairs with any setting

niche.reason: Soft iridescent sheen with a natural link to moon-phase charging traditions — differentiates from generic lists
niche.compliance_phrase: A stone many choose for its soft iridescent sheen and connection to lunar ritual traditions

safety_warning.role_label: The Cautionary Example (Why Sun Safety Matters)
safety_warning.reason: Color fades with prolonged direct sunlight — the textbook case for why 'check sun tolerance before sunlight charging' is the headline message
safety_warning.compliance_phrase: A stone many keep on their nightstand as part of a wind-down ritual before bed

jewelry_scene.reason: A common bracelet stone — wear as a daily reminder; needs no-contact charging (moonlight/selenite) to protect color from sun fade
jewelry_scene.compliance_phrase: A rose quartz bracelet many people wear as a daily reminder to be gentle with themselves

data_snapshot.mineral_safety_reference_version: v1.0 (14 stones, sun_safe列校准 — amethyst/rose-quartz=avoid, citrine/clear-quartz=indirect)
data_snapshot.verified_by: 数据一致性 diff + 人工抽检

> 💡 **Not sure if your stone can go in sunlight?**
> Enter your stone name in the **[Crystal Cleansing Timer](/tools/crystal-cleansing-timer/)** →
> check its sun/water/salt safety, get the recommended charging method, and start a guided countdown.

insertion_points.M3-moonlight: 满月夜把手链/项链放窗台——整条charge不动，绳子/金属/石头都不受损（best no-contact charge for bracelets/necklaces）
insertion_points.M3-sunlight: 避免把amethyst/rose quartz手链放阳光下charge——颜色会褪；改用moonlight或selenite plate
insertion_points.M3-selenite: 首饰专长：把手链/项链放selenite plate上过夜charge即可，零接触零风险
insertion_points.M5-mistakes: 典型错误：'把手链放直射阳光下charge一整天' → amethyst/rose quartz褪色；正确做法moonlight/selenite
insertion_points.M6-which-hand: charge后佩戴：非主导手(接收手)traditionally suggested；标'traditionally'非规则
insertion_points.M7-faq: Can I charge my bracelet in sunlight? → 仅sun-stable石(citrine/clear quartz)且brief indirect；amethyst/rose quartz用moonlight

jewelry_angle.m6_role: summary + H7 deep-dive link（不深入bracelet材质细节，留H7专篇）

eastern_touch.moonlight: 满月冥想 / 月相周期 ritual (与moon-calendar工具联动，满月是最强charge时刻)
eastern_touch.sound: Tibetan singing bowl / 颂钵 / 铜磬 (站点实卖铜器，天然契合charge音钵)
eastern_touch.earth: 玉石文化'返璞归真' / 大地冥想 (瑜伽grounding语境)
eastern_touch.sunlight: Surya Namaskar (拜日式) / 日光仪式语境

M1_quick_answer: 最安全通用charge法 = moonlight / selenite plate / sound / crystal cluster
不是所有水晶能晒阳光，amethyst/rose quartz会褪色；不确定先别用阳光
charge前先cleanse（cleanse first charge second），进入方法前一句话'先确认阳光耐受性'
M2_order_hardware: 第一段(cleanse first charge second顺序): charge前必须先cleanse——清空杯子再倒水
第二段(为什么需要charge): 传统/象征意义(能量低时refill, 满月周期, dull感)
第三段(概念区分): Charge vs Cleanse局部对比(比喻清空杯子再倒水) + 内链H1完整四概念表
safety_first_narrative.compliance_phrasing: symbolic energy state / traditionally believed to / many people experience

howto_6_steps.name: Cleanse first (then charge)
howto_6_steps.text: charge前必须先cleanse——清空杯子再倒水；用moonlight/selenite/sound/smoke任一安全法先清

howto_6_steps.name: Check sun tolerance
howto_6_steps.text: 查mineral-safety-reference或cleansing-timer工具，确认该石能否晒阳光(amethyst/rose quartz=avoid)

schema.faq_page_schema_consistency: FAQ答案与Schema FAQPage严格一致（同数据块生成页面+schema）

avoid: fills the crystal with positive energy
compliance.gentle_note: Crystal charging practices are based on spiritual traditions, symbolism, and personal mindfulness. There is no scientific evidence that crystals store, deplete, or release energy, but the safety guidance (which stones tolerate sunlight) is real mineralogy — and the reason a mindful ritual can coexist with caring for your stones.
compliance.chemistry_case_sensitive: SiO₂（非sio₂）/ CaCO₃ / Fe₂O₃ — 校验用includes('sio₂')小写查真违规
